title: PUT /books/{id} returns 200 for a non-existent book
evidence: main.go:106-110 UpdateBook uses db.Exec, which never returns sql.ErrNoRows, so the 404 branch at main.go:391-393 (and handleUpdateBook at main.go:243) is dead — updating a missing ID reports 200 'Book updated successfully'
suggestion: Check RowsAffected() from the Exec result and return 404 when zero rows were updated

title: DELETE /books/{id} returns 200 for a non-existent book
evidence: main.go:112-115 DeleteBook uses db.Exec, so the sql.ErrNoRows 404 branch at main.go:405-406 (and handleDeleteBook at main.go:274) is dead — deleting a missing ID reports 200 'Book deleted successfully'
suggestion: Check RowsAffected() and return 404 when zero rows were deleted

title: Production /books/{id} handlers are untested duplicates of the tested ones
evidence: main() wires handleGetBookWithID/handleUpdateBookWithID/handleDeleteBookWithID (main.go:358-414), but main_test.go calls the standalone handleGetBook/handleUpdateBook/handleDeleteBook (main.go:176,207,254) — two parallel implementations, leaving the served path uncovered (test_coverage=0.452 from scores.json)
suggestion: Delete one set of handlers and route/test the single remaining implementation

title: PUT /books/{id} returns a status message instead of the updated resource
evidence: main.go:399 responds with {"message":"Book updated successfully"} rather than the updated Book object
suggestion: Return the updated Book as JSON for API consistency with POST/GET
